title: TNPSC 2026 — Tamil Nadu State Services Exams
meta_description: TNPSC Group 1/2/2A 2026 — Tamil Nadu PSC exams for Deputy Collector, DSP. Exam pattern, TN-specific syllabus, no negative marking, salary ₹36K-1.5L.

hero.one_liner: Tamil Nadu's state services exams — Group 1 for Deputy Collector/DSP, Group 2 for Sub-Registrar, Group 2A for entry-level positions.

what_is_it: TNPSC (Tamil Nadu Public Service Commission) conducts three levels of state services exams: Group 1 (highest — Deputy Collector, DSP, Assistant Commissioner), Group 2 (mid-level — Sub-Registrar, Municipal Commissioner, Probation Officer), and Group 2A (entry-level — Junior Inspector, Assistant Section Officer). Each has different eligibility and exam patterns. Many aspirants start with Group 2A, progress to Group 2, then attempt Group 1 — a progressive approach building experience.

Group 1 is TNPSC's premier exam. Prelims: 300 MCQs in 3 hours (GS + Aptitude combined, NO negative marking). Mains: 3 papers (each 3 hours). Interview. The critical feature: TNPSC has ZERO negative marking across ALL its exams — attempt every single question. This is unique among PSCs and fundamentally changes strategy. Tamil Nadu-specific content (25-30% of questions): Chola/Pandya history, Dravidian movement, Periyar, Tamil language literature, TN geography (Western Ghats, Nilgiris, rivers), state economy (auto hub, IT corridor), and current affairs.

TNPSC is known for objective, merit-based selection with minimal controversy. Exams are conducted in both Tamil and English. Group 1 salary at Level 14-17 (TN scale): ₹80,000-1,20,000/month. Group 2 at Level 11-14: ₹50,000-70,000/month. Group 2A entry-level: ₹35,000-55,000/month. TNPSC notification for 2026: Group 2A exams ongoing/completed, Group 1 scheduled September 6, 2026, Group 2 scheduled October 25, 2026.

TNPSC's advantage: NO negative marking on ANY exam (unique feature). Even random guessing on 30% of questions gives free marks. This makes TNPSC relatively more achievable vs other PSCs where penalties prevent guessing. Tamil Nadu-specific advantages: Tamil medium students fluent in Tamil score 10-15% higher. If Tamil is your mother tongue, RC in Tamil is easier than English translation.

exam_pattern.negative_marking: ZERO negative marking. +1 for each correct answer.

extra_sections.heading: NO NEGATIVE MARKING — Your Biggest Strategic Advantage
content:
TNPSC's Zero Penalty Approach

This is TNPSC's defining feature — every wrong answer costs ZERO marks. Contrast: In UPSC, wrong answer = -1/3 mark.
In IBPS, wrong answer = -0.25. In TNPSC, wrong answer = 0.
This means: Random guessing has positive expected value. If you attempt 100 questions you don't know: 25% correct by chance = 25 marks.
Why leave them blank? Never leave a question unattempted in TNPSC.
Even if you've read only 200 out of 300 questions thoroughly, fill bubbles for all 300 with your best guesses on the rest.

Impact on Preparation & Exam Strategy

This changes everything: (1) You can take calculated risks — attempt any question where you've read some context, (2) Speed matters less (no penalty for wrong), accuracy matters more (gain marks for correct), (3) Preparation can be broader but shallower than UPSC (300 questions × no penalty = can afford 60% accuracy), (4) Last-minute guessing has value (unlike other exams where it's risky).
Example: You score 55% (165/300) in TNPSC = 99 percentile.

extra_sections.heading: TN-Specific Preparation Topics
content:
Tamil Nadu History (20-25% of questions)

Ancient: Sangam literature period, Chola dynasty (Rajaraja I, Rajendra Chola — maritime empire), Pandyas (southern kingdom), Pallavas (Mahabalipuram temples, art). Medieval: Vijayanagara empire in TN, Nayak rulers of Madurai/Thanjavur.

Modern & Contemporary: Justice Party (social reform), Self-Respect Movement (Periyar/E.V.R.), Dravidian ideology (Anna, Karunanidhi, MGR, Jayalalithaa). Independence struggle: Tilak, Aurobindo's presence in Pondicherry, 1857 participation.
Formation of modern Tamil Nadu (1956-present). Questions test exact details: 'Chola empire capital?' 'Periyar's birth year?' 'Dravidian movement founder?'

Tamil Nadu Geography (15-20% of questions)

Western and Eastern Ghats, Nilgiris (Ooty, Coonoor, Kodaikanal), Western coastal zone, Eastern coastal plains. Major rivers: Kaveri (dispute with Karnataka), Thamirabarani, Palar, Cooum.
Water resources: Mettur Dam, Vaigai Dam, Krishna-Godavari deltas. Climate zones: Tropical, sub-tropical, desert (south).
Soil types: Alluvial in deltas, red/laterite in Nilgiris. Wildlife: Annamalai tiger reserve, Mudumalai Sanctuary, Gulf of Mannar marine sanctuary, Palk Strait.

TN Economy & Current Affairs

Auto hub: Chennai (Detroit of Asia), 40% of India's automobile manufacturing. IT sector: Sholinganallur-Siruseri corridor (thousands of IT companies).
Textiles: Tiruppur cotton industry, Coimbatore weaving. Agriculture: Paddy (rice bowl of India), sugarcane, banana, spices.
Industries: Petrochemical in Chennai, steel, cement. Major government schemes: Dr.
Ambedkar Hatchery scheme, welfare programs. State budget, recent infrastructure projects, political developments.

extra_sections.heading: Group 1 vs Group 2 vs Group 2A — Progressive Approach
rows: Get government job → experience → appear for Group 2/1

preparation_tips: Attempt EVERY SINGLE QUESTION in TNPSC exams. Zero negative marking means no question should be left blank. If unsure, mark your best guess — 25% chance of correctness = net positive score. This is the opposite of UPSC strategy where blank is safer than wrong.

NCERT + TN state board textbooks are foundation. Samacheer Kalvi textbooks for Classes 6-12 Tamil, History, Geography directly feed into TNPSC questions. These textbooks are TN's official curriculum — TNPSC draws heavily from them. Read state board History and Geography books cover-to-cover.

Start with Group 2A (easier, more vacancies). Group 2A exams are annual/biennial. Getting Group 2A in first attempt builds confidence and ensures government job. Then prepare for Group 2 while working as Group 2A officer. Finally, target Group 1 with experience. This pyramid approach is safer than jumping to Group 1 directly.

Tamil medium students have significant advantage. TNPSC Tamil translation of questions is often clearer than English. If you're comfortable in Tamil, write the exam in Tamil. You'll find RC easier in Tamil than English version. This 10-15% advantage can be decisive between 95 and 99 percentile.

Previous year TNPSC papers (2010-2025, 15 years) are your best resource. Solve all available papers multiple times. Questions repeat frequently across years — sometimes identical, sometimes with slight variations. After 4-5 complete revisions of previous papers, you'll recognize 20-25% of actual exam questions.

faqs.question: Group 1 vs Group 2 vs Group 2A — which should I prepare for?
faqs.answer: Prepare for the highest level you can target. All three have overlapping syllabi. However, Group 2A is easier (lower cutoff, more vacancies). Strategic approach: If you're weak in certain topics, start with Group 2A for guaranteed success. If confident, prepare for Group 1 directly. Remember: Group 2A passing helps your resume; Group 1 passing gives you prestige. Many candidates appear for all three simultaneously or sequentially.

faqs.question: TNPSC no negative marking — confirmed for all exams?
faqs.answer: Yes. This is TNPSC's consistent policy across Group 1, Group 2, Group 2A, Group 4, and all departmental exams. No exception. This zero-negative policy is unique among India's major PSCs. Always verify latest notification, but this has been TNPSC's tradition for decades.

faqs.question: I don't speak Tamil. Can I appear for TNPSC?
faqs.answer: Yes. TNPSC exams available in both Tamil and English. However, Group 1 Mains has a compulsory Tamil paper testing basic Tamil language proficiency. If you have zero Tamil knowledge, this paper is challenging. For Group 2A (no Mains), Tamil knowledge is irrelevant — appear in English, no problem. Suggestion: Learn basic Tamil (6 months) or accept that Tamil paper will be weak in Group 1 Mains.

faqs.question: I scored 88% in Group 2A. Can I get government job?
faqs.answer: 88% in TNPSC with no negative marking is approximately 264/300 marks = 99+ percentile. Yes, you'll definitely get Group 2A appointment. Exact position (Sub-Inspector vs Junior Inspector vs ASO) depends on which service/department has vacancies, but you're very likely to get a government job.

syllabus.topics: Indian history, geography, polity, economics. Tamil Nadu-specific: TN history (Chola, Pandya, Pallava dynasties, Dravidian movement), TN geography (Western Ghats, Cauvery basin), TN culture (Bharatanatyam, Pongal, temple architecture), TN economy (IT, automobile hub)

study_plan.tasks: Read NCERTs for History, Geography, Polity. Start Tamil Nadu state GK book. Study state-specific history, geography, culture, and current schemes. Daily newspaper reading for current affairs.
Laxmikanth Polity, Ramesh Singh Economy. Deep dive into Tamil Nadu-specific topics — this is what separates State PSC from UPSC. Start answer writing practice for Mains (250 words).
Solve last 10 years TNPSC papers. Weekly full mocks. Focus on Tamil Nadu current affairs and state government policies. Interview preparation alongside.
